comment: E07 upstream security model example (docs/mcp-upstream-security-model.md). Three upstreams at three trust levels. 'comment' fields are annotations only.

upstreams.name: github
upstreams.comment: Reviewed first-party server launched via the known runner npx -> local-trusted. Forwards exactly one secret by NAME from the local environment; the value never appears in this file or any log.

tools.name: create_issue
tools.description: Create a GitHub issue in a repository

upstreams.name: filesystem
upstreams.comment: Third-party server at the default trust level local-restricted: absolute command path required, nothing forwarded beyond the base environment, explicit absolute cwd, tight response cap (within the restricted ceiling of 1048576).

upstreams.name: hosted-search
upstreams.comment: Reserved slot for a future remote upstream. Every operation on it is refused with trust_level_violation (-32010) until the OAuth/remote gate opens; enabled:false keeps it inert either way. The command is a placeholder that is never executed.
